core.label: Java Fundamentals
core.description: JDK setup, syntax, primitive types, and basic program structure.

topics.label: JDK Installation & IDEs
topics.description: Install the JDK and set up an IDE (IntelliJ or VS Code). The JDK includes the compiler, JVM, and standard library.

topics.label: Hello World & Program Structure
topics.description: Understand the main method, classes as entry points, and compilation with javac. Every Java program lives inside a class.

topics.label: Primitive Types & Variables
topics.description: int, long, double, float, char, boolean, byte, short. Java has strict typing with no implicit narrowing.

topics.label: Operators & Expressions
topics.description: Arithmetic, comparison, logical, bitwise, and ternary operators. Foundation of all program logic.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else, switch (including switch expressions), for, while, do-while, break, continue. Standard flow control.

topics.label: Strings
topics.description: String class, immutability, StringBuilder, and common methods. Strings are objects in Java, not primitives.

topics.label: Arrays
topics.description: Fixed-size typed arrays and multi-dimensional arrays. Arrays are the simplest data structure in Java.

topics.label: Methods
topics.description: Define methods with parameters, return types, and overloading. Methods are how you organize logic in Java.

topics.label: Packages & Imports
topics.description: Organize classes into packages and use import statements. Packages prevent naming conflicts and structure projects.

topics.label: Type Casting & Wrapper Classes
topics.description: Cast between types and use Integer, Double, etc. for boxing/unboxing. Wrappers bridge primitives and objects.

core.label: Object-Oriented Programming
core.description: Classes, inheritance, polymorphism, interfaces, and encapsulation.

topics.label: Classes & Objects
topics.description: Define classes with fields, constructors, and methods. Everything in Java revolves around objects.

topics.label: Constructors & this
topics.description: Default, parameterized, and copy constructors. this refers to the current instance.

topics.label: Encapsulation & Access Modifiers
topics.description: private, protected, public, and package-private. Encapsulation hides implementation details.

topics.label: Inheritance
topics.description: Extend classes with extends, use super, and understand the Object class. Java supports single class inheritance.

topics.label: Polymorphism
topics.description: Method overriding, dynamic dispatch, and the @Override annotation. Polymorphism enables flexible, extensible code.

topics.label: Abstract Classes
topics.description: Define partial implementations with abstract methods. Abstract classes provide a template for subclasses.

topics.label: Interfaces
topics.description: Define contracts with interface, default methods, and static methods. Interfaces enable multiple type inheritance.

topics.label: Enums
topics.description: Type-safe constants with fields, methods, and constructors. Java enums are full objects, not just integers.

topics.label: Records
topics.description: Immutable data carriers with auto-generated equals, hashCode, and toString. Records reduce boilerplate for DTOs.

topics.label: Sealed Classes
topics.description: Restrict which classes can extend a type. Sealed classes enable exhaustive pattern matching.

core.label: Collections & Generics
core.description: The Collections Framework, generics, and data structures.

topics.label: List (ArrayList, LinkedList)
topics.description: Ordered collections with index access. ArrayList is the most used collection in Java.

topics.label: Set (HashSet, TreeSet)
topics.description: Unique element collections. HashSet for O(1) lookups, TreeSet for sorted order.

topics.label: Map (HashMap, TreeMap)
topics.description: Key-value mappings. HashMap is the workhorse for fast lookups in Java.

topics.label: Queue & Deque
topics.description: FIFO queues and double-ended queues. Used for task scheduling, BFS, and buffering.

topics.label: Iterators & for-each
topics.description: Traverse collections with Iterator and enhanced for loops. The Iterable interface powers for-each.

topics.label: Generics Basics
topics.description: Parameterize classes and methods with type parameters. Generics provide compile-time type safety.

topics.label: Wildcards & Bounds
topics.description: Use ?, extends, and super for flexible generic types. Wildcards enable covariance and contravariance.

topics.label: Comparable & Comparator
topics.description: Define natural ordering and custom sorting. Essential for sorted collections and Arrays.sort.

topics.label: Collections Utility Class
topics.description: sort, shuffle, unmodifiableList, synchronizedList, and more. Utility methods for common collection operations.

topics.label: Immutable Collections
topics.description: List.of, Set.of, Map.of for unmodifiable collections. Immutable collections are safer and thread-friendly.

core.label: Exception Handling & I/O
core.description: Error handling, file operations, and serialization.

topics.label: Try/Catch/Finally
topics.description: Handle checked and unchecked exceptions. Java distinguishes between recoverable and programming errors.

topics.label: Custom Exceptions
topics.description: Create domain-specific exception classes extending Exception or RuntimeException.

topics.label: Try-with-Resources
topics.description: Auto-close resources implementing AutoCloseable. Eliminates resource leak bugs.

topics.label: File I/O with NIO.2
topics.description: Read and write files with Files, Path, and Paths. NIO.2 is the modern Java file API.

topics.label: Streams (InputStream/OutputStream)
topics.description: Byte and character streams for I/O operations. Foundation for reading network data and files.

topics.label: Buffered I/O & Readers/Writers
topics.description: BufferedReader, BufferedWriter, and PrintWriter for efficient text I/O.

topics.label: Serialization
topics.description: Serialize objects to bytes with Serializable. Understand why modern Java prefers JSON over native serialization.

topics.label: Working with JSON
topics.description: Parse and generate JSON with Jackson or Gson. JSON is the standard for API communication.

core.label: Functional Programming & Streams
core.description: Lambda expressions, Stream API, Optional, and functional interfaces.

topics.label: Lambda Expressions
topics.description: Anonymous functions for concise callback syntax. Lambdas transformed Java from verbose to expressive.

topics.label: Functional Interfaces
topics.description: Predicate, Function, Consumer, Supplier, and BiFunction. The building blocks for lambda-based APIs.

topics.label: Method References
topics.description: Shorthand for lambdas that call existing methods. String::toLowerCase is cleaner than s -> s.toLowerCase().

topics.label: Stream API Basics
topics.description: Process collections with filter, map, reduce, collect. Streams enable declarative data processing.

topics.label: Stream Collectors
topics.description: toList, toSet, toMap, groupingBy, partitioningBy, joining. Collectors transform stream results.

topics.label: Optional
topics.description: Avoid null with Optional<T>. Optional forces explicit handling of absent values.

topics.label: Parallel Streams
topics.description: Process streams in parallel with .parallel(). Easy parallelism but requires understanding of thread safety.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: Pattern matching for instanceof and switch (Java 21+). Reduces casting boilerplate and enables exhaustive checks.

core.label: Concurrency
core.description: Threads, executors, synchronization, and modern concurrency.

topics.label: Threads & Runnable
topics.description: Create threads with Thread and Runnable. Understanding threads is essential for server-side Java.

topics.label: Synchronization & Locks
topics.description: synchronized blocks, ReentrantLock, and volatile. Prevent data races in shared-state concurrency.

topics.label: ExecutorService & Thread Pools
topics.description: Manage thread lifecycles with Executors. Thread pools avoid the overhead of creating threads per task.

topics.label: CompletableFuture
topics.description: Compose async operations with thenApply, thenCompose, and allOf. Modern async programming in Java.

topics.label: Concurrent Collections
topics.description: ConcurrentHashMap, CopyOnWriteArrayList, BlockingQueue. Thread-safe collections for concurrent access.

topics.label: Virtual Threads (Project Loom)
topics.description: Lightweight threads for massive concurrency (Java 21+). Virtual threads simplify concurrent server code.

topics.label: Atomic Variables
topics.description: AtomicInteger, AtomicReference for lock-free thread-safe operations. Atomics are faster than synchronization.

topics.label: Structured Concurrency
topics.description: Manage concurrent subtask lifecycles as a unit (Java 21+ preview). Prevents thread leaks in complex async flows.

core.label: Build Tools, Testing & JVM
core.description: Maven/Gradle, unit testing, modules, and JVM internals.

topics.label: Maven
topics.description: Dependency management and build lifecycle with pom.xml. Maven is the most widely used Java build tool.

topics.label: Gradle
topics.description: Flexible build automation with Groovy/Kotlin DSL. Gradle is faster and more configurable than Maven.

topics.label: JUnit 5
topics.description: Write unit tests with @Test, assertions, and lifecycle hooks. JUnit is the standard Java testing framework.

topics.label: Mockito
topics.description: Mock dependencies for isolated unit tests. Mockito is the most popular Java mocking framework.

topics.label: JDBC Basics
topics.description: Connect to databases with JDBC, execute queries, and handle ResultSets. The foundation for all Java database access.

topics.label: Modules (JPMS)
topics.description: Modularize applications with module-info.java. JPMS provides strong encapsulation at the package level.

topics.label: JVM Internals Overview
topics.description: Class loading, bytecode, JIT compilation, and memory model. Understanding the JVM helps diagnose production issues.

topics.label: Garbage Collection
topics.description: GC algorithms (G1, ZGC, Shenandoah), tuning flags, and monitoring. GC knowledge is essential for Java performance.

topics.label: Debugging & Profiling
topics.description: Use debugger, JFR (Java Flight Recorder), and VisualVM. Profiling reveals bottlenecks in running applications.

topics.label: Logging (SLF4J + Logback)
topics.description: Structured logging with SLF4J API and Logback implementation. Proper logging is critical for production debugging.
